title: Shopify Website Operations Specialist
desc: Shopify Website Operations Specialist (E-Commerce) ***DO NOT CALL US*** ***APPLY THROUGH UPWORK ONLY*** Company: OneLaser Location: Remote (Must work Pacific Standard Time hours) Schedule: Monday–Friday, 9:00 AM PST – 5:45 PM PST Employment Type: Full-Time About OneLaser OneLaser is a fast-growing direct-to-consumer e-commerce company serving makers, creators, small businesses, and hobbyists with innovative laser engraving solutions. We’re looking for a highly organized and conversion-focused Shopify Website Operations Specialist to manage and optimize our online storefront while collaborating closely with our marketing, sales, and creative teams. Position Overview The Shopify Website Operations Specialist will be responsible for the day-to-day management, optimization, and growth of our Shopify storefront. This role combines website operations, conversion rate optimization, merchandising, landing page creation, promotional execution, and cross-functional communication. The ideal candidate understands how to create a seamless customer experience that drives revenue while ensuring all campaigns, promotions, and website updates are executed accurately and on schedule. Responsibilities Shopify Store Management * Manage and maintain the OneLaser Shopify storefront. * Create, update, and optimize product pages, collections, and navigation. * Ensure website content is accurate, current, and aligned with marketing initiatives. * Monitor site performance and proactively identify areas for improvement. Landing Page Development * Build high-converting landing pages for product launches, promotions, events, and advertising campaigns. * Coordinate with marketing and design teams to ensure consistent branding and messaging. * Implement best practices for user experience and conversion optimization. Campaign & Promotion Execution * Launch and manage website promotions, discounts, bundles, and seasonal campaigns. * Coordinate with internal marketing teams to ensure timely execution of all campaigns. * QA all promotional content before launch to ensure accuracy. Conversion Rate Optimization (CRO) * Analyze website performance, customer behavior, and sales funnels. * Recommend and implement improvements to increase conversion rates and average order value. * Conduct A/B testing and performance analysis where applicable. * Optimize product pages, collection pages, landing pages, and checkout experiences. Website Analytics & Reporting * Monitor key performance metrics including: * Conversion Rate * Average Order Value (AOV) * Revenue per Visitor * Bounce Rate * Cart Abandonment Rate * Provide regular performance reports and recommendations. Cross-Functional Communication * Collaborate daily with marketing, creative, sales, and leadership teams. * Attend internal meetings during Pacific Time business hours. * Communicate project status, website updates, and campaign readiness. Requirements Required Experience * 2+ years of Shopify website management experience. * Strong understanding of e-commerce best practices. * Experience building Shopify landing pages and promotional campaigns. * Experience working with Shopify themes and page builders. * Familiarity with conversion rate optimization principles. * Experience coordinating with marketing teams. Technical Skills * Shopify Admin * Shopify Apps & Integrations * Landing Page Builders (PageFly, GemPages, Replo, or similar) * Google Analytics * Google Tag Manager (preferred) * Basic HTML/CSS knowledge * Klaviyo experience is a plus Soft Skills * Excellent written and verbal English communication. * Strong attention to detail. * Ability to manage multiple projects simultaneously. * Self-motivated and highly organized. * Ability to work independently while collaborating with a remote team. Work Hours Requirement This position requires availability during our core business hours: Monday–Friday 9:00 AM PST – 5:45 PM PST Applicants must be available for meetings, project collaboration, and real-time communication during these hours. Success Metrics Success in this role will be measured by: * Website conversion rate improvements * Successful execution of campaigns and promotions * Landing page performance * Website uptime and accuracy * Revenue growth from website initiatives * Timely completion of assigned projects How to Apply Please submit: 1. Resume 2. Portfolio or examples of Shopify stores you’ve managed 3. Examples of landing pages you’ve built 4. Brief summary of your Shopify experience 5. Confirmation that you can work 9:00 AM PST – 5:45 PM PST

title: Senior QA Engineer (Strong Automation, Playwright & AI Tools Experience)
desc: Position open for a skilled QA Engineer with experience in B2C and SaaS applications to lead both manual and automated testing across web, mobile, and desktop platforms. You should be confident writing automation scripts in Playwright, and familiar with using Cursor or GitHub Copilot to accelerate test development. What you will do: – Own the QA process across platforms: Design, write, and execute test plans for features across web, desktop, and mobile applications. Coordinate with product managers and developers to understand feature goals and define test coverage. Think critically about edge cases, usability, and reliability. Raise issues early and validate fixes quickly. – Write and maintain automated tests: Use Playwright, Cypress, and Selenium to write clean, maintainable automation scripts. Use tools like Cursor or GitHub Copilot to speed up and improve test development. Ensure test coverage is optimized for core functionality, regressions, and edge cases. – Validate performance, security, and stability: Conduct load testing, performance benchmarking, and security testing to ensure applications scale and meet reliability standards. Test across environments including AWS and GCP, and coordinate results with dev teams to implement improvements. Our technologies: Playwright / Cypress / Selenium Postman / REST APIs Cursor / GitHub Copilot AWS / GCP Requirements: - 5+ years of professional experience in QA for B2C and SaaS applications. - Solid experience in manual and automation testing. - Proven ability to write and maintain automation tests using Playwright, Cypress, and Selenium. - Experience using Cursor or GitHub Copilot to assist with test development. - Strong experience with Postman and API testing. - Familiarity with cloud platforms like AWS and GCP. - Experience with load, performance, and security testing. - Able to test across web, mobile, and desktop platforms. - Excellent communication and documentation skills in English. Necessary strengths: 1 - Organization and Planning – Anticipate testing needs, define test coverage, and manage test cases efficiently. 2 - Proactivity – Don’t wait for test cases to be assigned—actively look for issues and process improvements. 3 - Attention to Detail – Identify even minor defects that could impact user experience or performance. 4 - Communication – Write bug reports clearly and concisely. Keep the team informed on test progress and issues. Important notes: NO AGENCIES – Only independent freelancers will be considered. Please include A LINK TO THE PDF FORMAT OF YOUR RESUME and examples of test automation you’ve written—especially using Playwright—and describe how you’ve used AI tools like Cursor or Copilot to assist in your QA workflows.

title: Frontend Specialist for AI Automated workflow to make Presentations easily editable
desc: Overview I publish presentation / landing-page-style decks online and need a clean, reliable way for non-technical colleagues and clients to edit them, mainly text and images, without touching code or breaking the layout. Edits should save to a single shared version so everyone sees the same up-to-date page live. Before any building happens, I want an expert to recommend the best approach. Scope of This Engagement This is consultation-first. The initial deliverable is your expert recommendation on the right architecture and tooling for the goal above, along with the trade-offs. If your recommendation is a good fit, there's a strong possibility of a follow-on build engagement to implement it, but the first priority is sound advice, not code. What I'd Like You to Advise On The best overall approach for making published, landing-page-style web pages editable by non-technical people. How to let users edit content only (text and images) while keeping the design, layout, and styling locked so they can't break it. Where the editable content should be stored, and how a single shared "source of truth" is maintained so everyone sees the same version. How edits get published and go live (instant vs. review/approval, draft vs. published states). Realistic cost: setup, any licensing/subscription fees, and ongoing running costs. Ongoing maintenance and reliability: how much upkeep this needs and who can do it. WordPress is one option I'm considering, but I'm open to others. Compare WordPress against the alternatives (e.g. headless CMS, inline/visual editing tools, page builders, SaaS deck/site tools, static sites with a lightweight editor) and tell me what you'd choose and why. Required Skills & Experience Strong front-end background (HTML, CSS, JavaScript) and an eye for keeping published pages pixel-stable. Hands-on experience building or configuring systems where non-technical users edit live web content. Working knowledge of multiple CMS / editing approaches, including WordPress and at least one headless or inline-editing alternative. Solid grasp of content storage, publishing workflows, and "single shared version" mechanics. Ability to weigh cost, maintenance, and ease-of-use trade-offs and explain them in plain language. Clear written and verbal communication; comfortable giving a straight recommendation. Nice-to-Have Experience with non-technical client handover and writing simple editing guides or documentation. Familiarity with image handling/optimization for web (uploads, sizing, performance). Exposure to access control / user permissions for editors. A portfolio of live sites or decks that non-technical people actively maintain themselves.

title: Urgent: Proofreader/Editor for WWII History Project
desc: Job Description: We are seeking an experienced publishing professional to perform a strict error-correction pass on a high school student's project book focusing on the Doolittle Raid and the subsequent China rescue during World War II. Important Note: We are not looking for stylistic rewriting, developmental editing, or polishing. The primary goal is to correct objective errors while preserving the student's original voice and narrative structure. Project Scope & Structure: This project consists of two distinct phases spaced a few weeks apart. Phase 1: Strict Text Error Correction (Immediate) Task: Correct all errors in grammar, punctuation, spelling, and typos in the raw manuscript (~13,300 words). Ensure consistency in historical names and terms. Timeline: Strict 3-day turnaround from the official hire date. Phase 2: Final PDF Page Proof Review (Approx. 20+ days later) Task: A final, quick proofreading pass on the designed page proofs (PDF layout) to check for formatting, and to ensure the 20+ illustrations and captions are correctly aligned and error-free before going to print. Timeline: This will happen at least 20 days later once the book layout is complete. (Turnaround for this final pass: 24–48 hours upon receiving the PDF). Budget & Milestones ($450 Fixed Price): The total budget will be split into two Upwork milestones to reflect the timeline: Milestone 1 ($350): Released upon successful delivery of the clean Phase 1 manuscript within the 3-day deadline. Milestone 2 ($100): Activated and released upon completion of the final PDF page proof review (20+ days later). Required Qualifications: 5+ years of experience in the book publishing industry. An eagle eye for typos, grammar, and punctuation mistakes. Familiarity with standard publishing style guides (e.g., Chicago Manual of Style) and final layout inspection. Immediate availability for Phase 1 and highly responsive communication. If you are a detail-oriented professional who can deliver clean, accurate results quickly and is willing to sync back up for the final print check in a few weeks, please apply. Please briefly state your availability for both phases in your proposal.

title: Adobe InDesign Textbook Formatting & Digitization
desc: We are seeking an experienced InDesign specialist to format and digitize a new textbook, approximately 100 pages, for both digital "ebook" PDF and print-ready formats. The primary goal is to transform the provided Google Doc content into polished, professional InDesign files, ensuring consistent formatting, optimized imagery, and functionality for various distribution channels. Project Deliverables Digital eBook PDF: Textbook formatted as a high-quality PDF with all links (e.g., table of contents, internal references, external URLs) made live and clickable. This PDF should be optimized for digital viewing. Print-Ready File: Textbook formatted as a black and white PDF suitable for upload to the Lulu.com book distribution platform. These files must adhere to print specifications (e.g., bleed, margins, image resolution) to ensure high-quality physical production. All Project Source Files: This includes the complete InDesign project file (.indd, .idml), linked assets (images, fonts), and any other associated files created during the project. These files are crucial for future in-house updates and revisions. Project Scope & Responsibilities Content Ingestion: Importing text content from the provided Google Docs into InDesign. Formatting & Layout: Applying consistent styling and layout based on the provided samples. This includes managing headings, body text, lists, tables, and other textual elements. Image Integration & Optimization: Placing all provided images from Google Drive into the InDesign files, ensuring proper resolution, sizing, and placement for both digital and print output. This may involve basic image adjustments if necessary for clarity. Hyperlink Creation: Identifying and creating all necessary live links within the digital ebook PDFs. Print Preparation: Preparing the files specifically for Lulu.com, including setting up correct page sizes, bleeds, margins, and ensuring all fonts are embedded, and images are suitable for black and white print. Quality Assurance: Thoroughly reviewing both the digital PDF and print-ready files for formatting errors, broken links, image quality issues, and overall consistency. Provided Resources We will provide the following assets to facilitate your work: Existing Textbook File: Template of final product from our most recent publication. Sample Textbook Pages: Sample pages from a textbook design that we would like to replicate components of for an updated, engaging layout. Text Content: in Google Docs format. Image Assets: All necessary images stored in organized folders on Google Drive. Technical Requirements Software: Proficiency and access to Adobe InDesign are essential. Timeline & Budget Timeline: We anticipate an initial delivery of the book within 1 week. An additional week will be allocated for any necessary edits or revisions after initial review. This will ideally be finalized by June 12, 2026. Budget: This is a fixed-price project with a total budget of $500 USD. Communication & Collaboration Primary Communication: Email and Google Chat will be our main methods of communication. We look forward to reviewing proposals from talented InDesign specialists who can help us bring these textbooks to life in professional digital and print formats.

title: AI UGC Video Editor 📍 Remote | $1,500–$2,500/month + Winning Ad Bonuses
desc: Summary As a video editor working for DTC brands, instead of actually seeing your work perform — You often become the "Head of Delivering Files" instead. From cutting briefs with no context, never seeing the data, juggling multiple clients, and getting feedback like "can you make the hook pop more"… Actually knowing if your creative works becomes nearly impossible. We know because we've been there. 🚨 And Spoiler Alert: It wasn't for us. So, instead of editing into a black hole — what if every concept you cut came with real numbers? Hook rate. Retention curve. What scaled and why. What died in 48 hours and exactly why. You know that feeling when you nail a hook and just know it's going to stop the scroll? Imagine getting to find out if you were right. Every single time. Fun right? Well, that's exactly what the AI UGC Video Editor role at our brand is all about. Working directly alongside our Creative Director to produce winning AI UGC ads — for a 7-figure women's fashion brand that has hit $100K days on Meta — while building the skills and knowledge to eventually grow into a Creative Strategist role. So, if this sounds like your kind of thing, and you like the idea of being a true creative partner with a real path to strategy — not just an executor — This opportunity is for you. About Us We're a 8-figure women's fashion brand that runs entirely on Meta. Every dollar of revenue has come from the creative. We're lean, AI-first, and obsessed with what actually works on paid social. Unlike brands that hand editors a script and say "make it look good" — we treat creative as the #1 growth lever. That means every editor on this team works directly with our Creative Director, sees real performance data, and has a voice in what gets made. We also invest heavily in our people. Every editor on this team gets daily access to Ecom Talent — one of the best video editing and creative training platforms in e-commerce — and the support of Evolve, one of the top marketing consulting groups in the industry. We pay for it. You grow from it. The better our creative gets, the faster we scale. That's the only way we grow. The Role: What You'll Own As our AI UGC Video Editor, you'll work directly with our Creative Director, Jacob, to produce 1–2 finished AI UGC concepts per day. You're not just cutting briefs — you're a creative partner. You flag weak scripts before they get edited. You bring hook ideas nobody asked for. You treat every concept like it has a real shot at scaling. Your Core Responsibilities: 1. Creative Execution Produce 1–2 finished AI UGC ad concepts per day, every working day Craft 3+ scroll-stopping hook variations per concept (minimum — not a suggestion) Structure every ad: hook → problem → solution → proof → CTA Add captions, sound design, music, and pattern interrupts that hold attention past the hook Turn around revisions within 12–24 hours — no chasing 2. AI Production Produce AI UGC using HeyGen, Arcads, Kling, ElevenLabs, Captions, Claude, Runway Output must look indistinguishable from real footage Source B-roll, stock, and UGC assets independently — don't wait to be handed everything 3. Growth & Learning Complete a weekly 30-minute ad review — your concepts vs. what's winning in market Watch 1 hour of Ecom Talent training daily (built into paid hours) Study competitor ads and trending formats weekly What We're Looking For We're looking for more than a typical "video editor" — we need a performance-obsessed creative who thinks like a marketer and is hungry to grow into strategy. Must-Have Experience: Already using AI tools daily: HeyGen, Arcads, Kling, ElevenLabs, Runway, or equivalent — not "willing to learn" Can show real AI UGC examples that look indistinguishable from real footage 1+ year editing DTC Meta ads: AI UGC, UGC, VSLs, B-roll voiceover Proficient in CapCut or Adobe Premiere Zero side clients — full attention only Must-Have Attributes: Hooks Obsessed: You live in the first 1–5 seconds Ownership-Driven: You don't wait to be told every step Data Over Ego: Performance over personal attachment to your work Organized: Clean files, named projects, nothing gets lost Compensation & Perks Base: $1,500–$2,500/month based on experience and output quality Winning Ad Bonuses — uncapped, rate disclosed in interview Daily Ecom Talent training built into paid hours — we pay for it Access to Evolve, one of the top marketing consulting groups in e-commerce Remote & fully async-flexible Creative Strategist growth path — for the right person who performs, this is where we want to take you 📓 Our Hiring Process Submit a 3-minute Loom: show your best AI UGC ad, walk through your hook choice, and tell us what AI tools you're actively using today. Participate in an Intro Call with our Creative Director. Complete a Paid Test — one AI UGC brief, 48 hours, paid. Offer. We will not respond to proposals without a Loom. We've built this brand to be the place performance editors come to grow into the best creative minds in DTC. If you're ready to do work you can actually track — and build a career doing it… Hit apply.

title: Graphic Designer to edit graphics needing additional work, and/or create new graphics

Some are subtle changes and some may require starting from scratch and incorporating the stated edits. We are on a short timeline and would like to receive the work within days rather than weeks. The designer will work independently, and our team will be available for questions and discussion as needed. Questions and requests for clarification are encouraged. The existing photos depict what we are attempting to accomplish but need to be scaled appropriately and details polished to tell the story more succinctly. 1) The first photo depicts a military troop interrogating a room using our technology (enclosed in the logoed case) from outside the building. In the existing photo, the concrete wall looks like a window. It should appear as a solid wall that we can see through to the contents inside the room. The suitcase should be shorter and longer to look like it's 5'-6' long and should be positioned on the ground outside the building near the center of the solid wall. Add spherical waves to depict a pulse emitting from the case, through the wall into the room. In addition to the existing device on the table, add some brown bricks on the table (to represent fentanyl or other types of drugs/contraband) or a jar of uranium. The soldier should appear to be positioned 3' to 6' from the case holding the computer, as shown. 2) The existing photo is of a golden dome that spans the entire United States, coast to coast. Include in the graphic some of the ocean surrounding the coastlines. Remove the city in the background. The dome should hover over the entire US and there should be a gap between the bottom of the dome and the map of the US. Do not include the dark strip at the bottom of the dome. The gap between the bottom of the dome and the map is intentional (since it cannot reach all the way to the land or ocean). The semi shown underneath provides context and should be included. We want to demonstrate that the gap creates a void that can be breached. This tells story of one of the capabilities of our technology. 3) This graphic shows the inside of the hull of a ship and the contents therein. It is being interrogated from the outside of the ship. The colored text corresponds to the colors assigned to the cargo in the hull and identifies each material. This graphic needs to be recreated. The contents can be anything from bananas to drugs, explosives, bombs, missiles, equipment, etc. Include a variety of cargo and overlay with a different color for each type of item.

title: Food Delivery App | React native (User app + Vendor app + admin panel)
desc: React Native Food Delivery App – Single Vendor (iOS + Android) We are a restaurant owner looking to build a custom branded food delivery app for our single restaurant chain operating across 3 locations. We want full ownership of the platform — no third-party marketplace like Swiggy or Zomato. Our customers order directly through our app. This is a fixed-scope project. We are not looking for hourly engagements. --- **What we're building:** Three apps + one admin panel: **Customer App (iOS + Android)** - Onboarding and OTP-based login (phone number) - Location detection and delivery radius check - Menu browsing by category with item customization (size, add-ons, spice level) - Cart management - Real-time order tracking with live driver location on map - Multiple payment methods — UPI, card, cash on delivery - Order history and reorder - Loyalty points system — earn points per order, redeem on next - Push notifications for order status updates - Ratings and reviews per order **Delivery Partner App (Android only)** - Login and profile setup - Accept or reject incoming orders - Navigation to restaurant and customer - Earnings summary per day/week - Availability toggle (online/offline) **Restaurant Dashboard (Web)** - Incoming order management — accept, prepare, ready - Menu management — add/edit/remove items, mark out of stock - Delivery partner assignment - Order history and revenue reports - Push notification controls **Admin Panel (Web)** - Manage all three locations - Customer management - Delivery partner onboarding and management - Promo codes and discount management - Revenue and order analytics across locations - Commission or payout management for delivery partners --- **Technical requirements:** - React Native for both customer and delivery apps - Web dashboard in React.js - Node.js backend with REST APIs - PostgreSQL database - Firebase for push notifications and OTP - Google Maps API for live tracking - AWS or equivalent cloud hosting - Payment gateway integration (Razorpay or Stripe) --- Here's that section updated: --- **Scope of work expected from the developer:** - Complete UI/UX design (we have brand guidelines — logo and color palette) - Frontend and backend development - API integrations (maps, payments, notifications) - App Store and Google Play submission - Full source code ownership transferred on final payment **Post-Launch Support & Maintenance:** We expect the developer to provide a clearly defined maintenance plan covering the following: - Bug fixes and crash resolution — response time expectations for critical vs minor issues - OS compatibility updates — keeping the app functional as iOS and Android release new versions - Third-party API maintenance — handling breaking changes from Google Maps, Firebase, payment gateway updates - Performance monitoring — server uptime, API response times, error tracking - Security patches — regular dependency updates and vulnerability fixes - Feature enhancement requests — how additional features outside original scope are scoped and priced - App Store compliance — handling policy changes from Apple and Google that require app updates **Please include in your proposal:** - Your monthly retainer cost for ongoing maintenance - What is covered under retainer vs billed separately - Your SLA (response time) for critical bugs affecting live orders - Whether the first 30/60/90 days post-launch are included in the project price or billed separately **Timeline expectation:** 12–14 weeks from kickoff **Required experience:** - React Native apps shipped to App Store and Google Play (send links) - Food delivery or marketplace app experience preferred - Firebase and Google Maps integration experience - Payment gateway integration experience --- **When applying please answer:** 1. Share two live App Store or Play Store links to apps you have shipped 2. Have you built a food delivery or similar real-time tracking app before? Briefly describe the project. 3. What is your approach to real-time driver location tracking — what technology would you use and why? Proposals that do not answer all three questions will not be reviewed.

Landing page for full context: https://123ceu.com/rcfe-meal-plan-subscription Scope of Work Review 12 months of monthly meal plans (menus + weekly shopping lists + step-by-step recipes) Verify nutritional adequacy and alignment with California 22 CCR §87555 (meal frequency, variety, modified diets, documentation) Confirm appropriate handling of the special diets listed below Provide a signed letter / written stamp of approval suitable for facility binders and LPA inspection Flag any items needing revision before sign-off Required Special-Diet Experience You must have hands-on experience reviewing menus across: Medical: Diabetic, Renal, Cardiac, Low-Sodium, Dysphagia, Mechanical Soft, Pureed, IDDSI 0–7, Gluten-Free, Low-FODMAP, Anti-Inflammatory, MIND Religious: Kosher, Halal, Catholic Lenten, Hindu Vegetarian, Buddhist Vegetarian, Ramadan Cultural: Mexican/Latin, Filipino, Mediterranean, Soul Food, Asian Allergy-Aware: Top-9 compliant, cross-contact aware Memory Care: Finger foods, hydration-boosting, dementia-friendly textures Behavioral/Youth: STRTP and trauma-informed meal patterns Requirements Must be a credentialed RD/RDN based in the United States (CDR registration number required) Prior experience with California RCFE, ARF, Group Home, or STRTP menu review strongly preferred Familiarity with Title 22 §87555 food service requirements Able to provide a signed approval document on professional letterhead Budget & Timeline Fixed price: $200 for full 12-month review and signed approval Turnaround: 2–3 weeks from receipt of materials To Apply, Please Include Your CDR registration number and state of practice 1–2 sentences on your RCFE / Title 22 / special-diet experience Confirmation you can issue a signed approval letter on letterhead

title: Social Media Manager/Content Creator for Two of Vancouver's Leading Cocktail Bars
desc: Job Description: Multi-Venue Social Media Manager & Content Creator About Us: We operate two of Vancouver’s premier upscale cocktail establishments: The Watson (Mount Pleasant)—a stylish, cocktail-forward venue with a stunning concrete, glass, and Victorian-library aesthetic—and Bartholomew (Yaletown), a sophisticated, intimate lounge known for its inventive drink program and the city's premier charcuterie and cheese experience. We are seeking an elite, highly creative Social Media Manager to take full ownership of both distinct brands. The Role: This hybrid position requires split weekly on-site content-capture shifts between Mount Pleasant and Yaletown, combined with remote strategy, editing, and community management. You will manage separate Instagram and TikTok channels for each brand, adapting your visual style to fit the unique neighborhood culture of both locations. Key Responsibilities: Dual-Brand Content Creation: Script, shoot, and edit high-quality Instagram Reels and TikToks. You must easily pivot from capturing the sleek, dramatic cocktail pours at The Watson to highlighting the intricate, artisanal details of Bartholomew's premium meat and cheese boards. Aesthetic Distinction: Maintain two completely distinct social grids. The Watson requires a moody, design-forward community vibe; Bartholomew demands an upscale, sophisticated Yaletown lounge aesthetic. Active Community Engagement: Monitor and manage daily direct messages, comments, reservation inquiries, and story tags across all accounts, fostering strong relationships with local guests, industry veterans, and food influencers. Strategic Campaigns: Coordinate promotions for distinct activations, such as wine pop-ups, late-night food menu features, daily happy hours, and holiday schedules for both rooms. What We Need: Vancouver Resident: Based locally with the ability to travel flexibly between Yaletown and Mount Pleasant for content shoots. Proven Hospitality Portfolio: Direct experience filming food and beverage content. You must understand how to utilize low-light conditions effectively to capture a cozy, romantic bar atmosphere. High-End Mobile Production: Mastery over short-form video editing applications (CapCut, Premiere Rush) and mobile lighting tools. Organization & Autonomy: Ability to manage two distinct content calendars simultaneously without crossing brand guidelines.

title: POS implementation
desc: We are looking for an experienced Toast POS / restaurant operations consultant to help optimize and fully implement Toast for a growing food truck business with multiple trucks. Our business currently uses Toast, but we are only using a small portion of what the system can do. We need someone who can help us take Toast to the next level and build a smarter operating system around it — including inventory, prep planning, event forecasting, reporting, and marketing opportunities. This is not just a basic Toast setup job. We need someone who understands food service operations, mobile food trucks, catering/event demand, inventory control, and how to use data to improve daily decisions. What We Need Help With Audit our current Toast setup and identify what is underused Set up or improve Toast inventory management Create prep-planning systems based on events, demand, and calendar bookings Connect sales history to future prep needs Help estimate how much food/labor is needed by event type Build reporting dashboards or weekly reports for management Organize menu items, modifiers, pricing, and item-level tracking Improve multi-truck operations inside Toast Recommend workflows for staff, managers, and owners Help identify marketing opportunities using Toast data Set up customer lists, loyalty, promotions, or email/SMS campaigns if available Train our team so the system is actually used correctly Ideal Candidate You should have experience with: Toast POS setup and optimization Restaurant, catering, or food truck operations Inventory systems and food cost control Demand forecasting and prep planning Event-based food service operations Restaurant marketing tools, loyalty, or customer segmentation Creating simple systems that busy teams will actually use Bonus if you have worked with multi-location restaurants, food trucks, catering businesses, or high-volume event-based food service. Goal We want Toast to become the central system that helps us answer: What do we need to prep this week? Which truck needs what inventory? What events are coming up? How much food should we produce based on demand? What menu items are most profitable? Where are we losing money or wasting inventory? How can we market better to existing customers? How can we scale operations across multiple trucks? Deliverables We would like the consultant to provide: Toast system audit Recommended setup changes Inventory and prep-planning workflow Reporting dashboard or weekly reporting structure Staff/manager training documentation Marketing and customer-data recommendations Implementation roadmap for the next 30–60 days To Apply Please include: Your experience with Toast POS Examples of restaurant, catering, or food truck businesses you have helped How you would approach optimizing Toast for a multi-truck operation Any experience with inventory, prep forecasting, or restaurant marketing Your hourly rate or fixed project recommendation We are looking for someone who can help us move fast, simplify operations, and make Toast a powerful tool for growth — not just a payment system.

We need a designer to: • Vectorise both logo versions into clean, production-ready .AI and .EPS master files • Produce the full logo suite as specified in our brand brief: - Primary (full colour on black) - Reversed (dark green on white/transparent) - Monochrome dark (all white on black) - Monochrome light (all black on white) - Icon only (crossed tees + ball, no letterforms) • Deliver all versions in .AI, .EPS, .SVG, .PDF and .PNG (300dpi + 72dpi, transparent and on-background) • Embroidery digitising files (.DST or equivalent) for all placement sizes across the collection will also be required — please indicate whether this is within your capability The logo features two crossed golf tees in deep forest green (Pantone 364 C), classical serif F and C letterforms, a white golf ball, and the wordmarks FAIRWAY COLLECTIVE and SWING DIFFERENT. Full colour references and brand guidelines are provided in the brief. --- WORKSTREAM 2 — TECH PACKS (4 STYLES + 1 PETITE VARIANT) We need professional tech packs produced for each of the following styles, ready to issue to a manufacturer: • FC-001 Short Sleeve Polo (performance piqué, white, forest green corozo buttons, sleeve stripe) • FC-002 Long Sleeve Top (ponte di roma, white, open V-neck, no buttons) • FC-003 Pull-On Trousers (performance stretch twill, porcini, drawstring waist, golf ball zip pull) • FC-003P Pull-On Trousers Petite (as FC-003, shorter inseam and adjusted rise) • FC-004 Visor (all white, Velcro strap) Each tech pack must include: • Front and back flat technical drawings (CAD) with all construction details called out • Measurement spec sheet across sizes XS–XL with grading increments • Construction detail callouts (seam types, stitch types, hem finishes) • Trims and notions detail page • Embroidery placement diagram for every logo placement position • Colourway page with fabric, thread, button, zip, and label colour references Full size specifications, fabric compositions, trims lists, embroidery specs, and construction notes are all provided in our manufacturing addendum. This is a well-documented brief — you will have everything you need. --- DELIVERABLES SUMMARY • Complete vector logo suite (all versions, all file formats) • 5 tech packs (FC-001, FC-002, FC-003, FC-003P, FC-004) • Embroidery digitising files (if within scope) --- BUDGET & PAYMENT MILESTONES Total budget: USD 800, structured as fixed-price milestones as follows: Milestone 1 — Project start: $200 (25%) Released on contract start. Covers initial briefing, logo image review, and commencement of vectorisation work. Milestone 2 — Logo suite delivered & approved: $200 (25%) Released on client approval of the complete vector logo suite in all specified formats. One round of revisions included. Milestone 3 — First two tech packs delivered & approved: $200 (25%) Released on client approval of FC-001 and FC-002 tech packs. One round of revisions per tech pack included. Milestone 4 — Remaining tech packs delivered & approved: $200 (25%) Released on client approval of FC-003, FC-003P, and FC-004 tech packs. One round of revisions per tech pack included. Embroidery digitising files (if in scope) delivered at this stage. Note: The budget breakdown assumes approximately $200–250 for the logo suite and $550–600 for the five tech packs. If embroidery digitising is within your capability, please indicate this in your proposal and confirm whether it is included within the $800 budget or priced separately. --- IDEAL CANDIDATE • Proven experience in apparel tech packs — please include portfolio examples • Strong Adobe Illustrator skills for both flat garment drawings and vector logo work • Experience working with small independent brands is a plus • Clear, professional communication and ability to work from a detailed written brief • Able to deliver within 3–4 weeks from project start --- TO APPLY Please include: 1. Examples of relevant tech pack work 2. Examples of logo vectorisation or brand identity work 3. Your proposed approach — in particular, can you handle both workstreams? 4. Your estimated timeline and cost breakdown by workstream 5. Confirmation of whether embroidery digitising is within your scope, and whether this is included in or additional to the stated budget 6. Any questions about the brief We will share the full brand brief and manufacturing addendum with shortlisted candidates before final selection.

title: OpenPLC & Node-RED Kiosk Image for Raspberry Pi 3B+ (Industrial Farm Deployment)
desc: I am seeking an experienced Automation / Embedded Systems Engineer to develop a robust, custom, production-ready Linux operating system image for a Raspberry Pi 3B+. The goal of this project is to create a 100% open-source, license-free "Gold Master" image that mimics a Unitronics smart relay/PLC workflow. This image will eventually be cloned across hundreds of offline poultry houses across a large-scale agricultural infrastructure. 🛠️ Hardware Stack (Target Environment) Controller: Raspberry Pi 3B+ booted completely offline (no internet access). Shield Stack: Sequent Microsystems Building Automation HAT + Sequent Stackable Smart UPS HAT (communicating via I2C). Display: Industrial HDMI Touchscreen. Power: 220V AC input stepped down via DIN-rail Mean Well power supplies (24V DC feeding the UPS HAT, separate line feeding the HDMI monitor). Outputs: On-board pilot relays triggering heavy-duty 220V AC industrial contactors/SSRs for high-voltage motors, heating, and lighting. 📋 Software & System Requirements Zero-Licensing Architecture: The entire system must utilize an open-source automation stack. We are using OpenPLC for the IEC 61131-3 visual ladder logic execution engine and Node-RED for the drag-and-drop HMI user interface. OS Hardening & Kiosk Mode: Built on Raspberry Pi OS Lite. The system must boot directly into a full-screen, fullscreen Chromium --kiosk browser pointing to the local Node-RED dashboard interface. There must be zero access to the underlying Linux terminal or desktop for farm operators. Display Power Management (DPMS): The system must utilize Linux screen blanking. The touchscreen backlight must completely power down after 5 minutes of inactivity and wake instantly back to the active HMI screen upon a physical touch event. Hardware Timekeeping (Offline): Synchronize the Linux system clock seamlessly on boot from the Sequent board’s battery-backed DS3231 Hardware RTC chip (hwclock) so the system maintains absolute real-world time accuracy without internet connectivity. Power Fail Resiliency & Retentive State: The software must listen to the Sequent UPS HAT. Upon 220V main power drop, a script must cleanly save current timer registers to retentive storage, write an event log, and execute a safe Linux shutdown to prevent SD card corruption. On power restoration, it must read the file and resume the cycle state seamlessly. HMI Password Protection: Implement a supervisor password/PIN keypad screen on the Node-RED interface, preventing unauthorized workers from modifying the timer settings. Data Logging & Audit Trail: Program local data logging (CSV or SQLite) to the SD card. It must record boot events, power losses, user logins, relay actions (start/stop), and track old vs. new values when configurations are edited. File writes must be buffered to optimize RAM and minimize SD card wear. Mass Replication Design: The image must be completely abstracted. All local variables (e.g., unique House ID, specific timer intervals) must be read from a local config file. Supervisors must be able to change these local configurations via the password-protected HMI screen so that the exact same master .img can be flashed onto hundreds of identical devices without code changes. 📦 Required Deliverables A single, universal flashable .img file ready to be duplicated onto standard SD cards. Clean, well-documented source code files for the OpenPLC logic and Node-RED flows. A highly clear, visual electrical wiring schematic showing exactly how the Pi, the Sequent HAT stack, the Mean Well power supplies, and the 220V contactors interconnect safely.

title: Part-Time Marketing Manager (Content, Social Media & Marketing Project Management)
desc: Overview We’re looking for a smart, organized, AI-savvy marketing consultant to support our B2B marketing team across content development, social media execution, and marketing project management. This is a generalist role, not a narrow specialist role. The right person is comfortable moving between writing, content repurposing, social media planning, campaign coordination, and project follow-through depending on what the team needs most each week. You’ll be a hands-on extension of our marketing team, helping turn ideas, conversations, and existing assets into polished marketing materials while keeping projects organized and moving forward. What You’ll Support 1. Content Development & Repurposing You’ll help turn existing ideas, outlines, transcripts, webinars, podcasts, and rough notes into polished marketing content. This may include: * Drafting blog posts, article outlines, and thought leadership content * Repurposing long-form content into social posts, email copy, and campaign assets * Adapting messaging for different channels, audiences, and formats * Using AI tools to speed up research, drafting, editing, and content workflows * Helping ensure content is clear, accurate, polished, and aligned with our brand voice 2. Social Media Management & Execution You’ll support social content planning and execution across key marketing channels. This may include: * Drafting LinkedIn posts and social captions from existing marketing materials * Repurposing blogs, webinars, podcasts, and campaigns into social content * Helping organize and maintain a social content calendar * Creating simple supporting visuals or content layouts in Canva or similar tools * Supporting publishing workflows and content distribution 3. Marketing Project Management You’ll help keep marketing initiatives organized, on track, and moving from idea to execution. This may include: * Managing timelines, deliverables, and next steps for marketing projects * Coordinating with internal team members and subject matter experts * Gathering inputs, organizing feedback, and helping move projects forward * Supporting webinars, events, speaking opportunities, and campaign execution * Researching conferences, podcasts, webinars, and other brand-building opportunities Sample Projects Examples of work may include: * Turning a webinar or podcast transcript into a blog post and several LinkedIn posts * Taking a rough outline and turning it into a polished content draft * Creating a weekly batch of social posts from existing thought leadership * Helping manage the timeline for a webinar or event marketing campaign * Drafting and formatting marketing emails in HubSpot * Researching speaking opportunities and organizing submission details * Supporting award submissions or promotional campaigns across email and social What We’re Looking For The ideal candidate is a strong marketing generalist who is organized, proactive, and comfortable using AI tools as part of their daily workflow. You should have: * Experience working in a B2B marketing environment * Strong writing, editing, and content development skills * Strong organization and project management skills * Experience supporting social media content, especially LinkedIn * Comfort managing multiple projects and moving pieces at once * Ability to work independently and communicate clearly * A fast-moving, detail-oriented working style * Experience using AI tools such as ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, Perplexity, or similar platforms to improve speed and quality Who You Are You’re someone who can take a rough idea and help turn it into something useful, polished, and ready to ship. You enjoy working across different areas of marketing rather than doing the same narrow task every day. You’re organized enough to keep projects moving, thoughtful enough to improve the work, and resourceful enough to figure things out without needing constant oversight. You don’t need to be a deep specialist in every channel. We’re looking for someone with good marketing instincts, solid writing skills, strong follow-through, and the ability to use AI and smart workflows to get high-quality work done efficiently. Engagement Details * Approximately 10 hours per week * Part-time consultant role * Initial short-term engagement with potential to extend * Remote * B2B marketing experience preferred To Apply Please answer the following: 1. Tell us about your experience working across content, social media, and marketing project management. 2. Share an example of a piece of content or social post you created or helped develop. Why did it work? 3. Walk us through how you’ve repurposed one content asset into multiple formats. 4. Share 2 AI tools you actively use and explain how you use them in your marketing workflow. 5. Describe a marketing project you helped manage or coordinate. What was your role in keeping it moving?

title: Spanish Localization Specialist & Translator
desc: # Spanish Localization Specialist & Translator (Technical Content) – Immediate Start ## Project Overview We are seeking an experienced Spanish Localization Specialist and Translator to support the localization of technical training and e-learning content for the skilled trades industry. This is a fast-paced project with aggressive deadlines, and we are looking for someone who can begin immediately and contribute with minimal onboarding. The ideal candidate has experience translating and localizing technical, instructional, and training materials from English into Spanish. Strong contextual understanding is essential, as much of the content is highly technical, simulation-based, and designed for real-world trade professionals. This role requires more than direct translation. The successful candidate must be able to adapt content naturally for Spanish-speaking audiences while preserving technical accuracy, instructional clarity, and industry-specific terminology. ## Key Responsibilities * Translate and localize English-language technical and training content into Spanish. * Adapt e-learning materials, presentations, subtitles, on-screen text, assessments, and supporting documentation. * Review and edit translated content to ensure consistency, quality, and adherence to terminology standards. * Collaborate with project managers, instructional designers, and other localization team members to meet tight project deadlines. * Localize images, graphics, and visual assets, including replacing and formatting translated text within images. * Conduct quality assurance reviews to ensure content is linguistically accurate, culturally appropriate, and technically precise. * Identify terminology, context, or localization issues and communicate recommendations to the project team. ## Required Qualifications * Native-level Spanish proficiency with excellent written communication skills. * Professional experience translating and localizing content from English into Spanish. * Demonstrated experience with technical, instructional, e-learning, or training content. * Familiarity with terminology related to one or more of the following industries: * Electrical * HVAC * Plumbing * Facilities Maintenance * Construction * Experience working with Google Workspace tools, including: * Google Slides * Google Drive * Shared folder management and organization * Ability to maintain formatting and layouts within presentation files during localization. * Experience localizing images and visual assets. * Basic video editing experience, including updating localized captions, subtitles, and on-screen text. * Strong attention to detail and ability to work effectively with context-heavy content. ## Project Requirements * Must be available to start immediately. * Must be comfortable working under tight deadlines and managing a high-volume workload. * Must be responsive and able to communicate effectively throughout the project. * Must be able to work independently while maintaining high-quality standards. * Previous experience with technical training, e-learning, or skilled trades content is strongly preferred. ## Preferred Qualifications * Experience localizing simulation-based learning content. * Familiarity with localization quality assurance (LQA) processes. * Experience working with terminology databases, style guides, and translation memory tools. * Background in the skilled trades industry or technical education. ## What We're Looking For We are looking for a localization professional who understands the importance of context, technical accuracy, and instructional clarity. The ideal candidate can confidently work with complex training materials, adapt content naturally for Spanish-speaking audiences, and consistently deliver high-quality work within a compressed project timeline.

title: Looking for a 1:1 Automation / SDET Mentor
desc: Hi, I’m looking for an experienced 1:1 Automation Engineer / SDET mentor who can help me strengthen my automation skills through practical, hands-on mentoring. I already come from a solid software testing background, and now I want to grow into a stronger automation-focused QA / SDET-style profile. My goal is not to attend generic training or slide-based sessions. I’m looking for someone who can guide me step by step, review my work, help me stay consistent, and build real capability over time. For now, I would like to start with an initial 1-month engagement and then continue further if the collaboration is a good fit. What I want to focus on in the first month I would like the first phase to cover practical foundations in areas such as: Playwright with TypeScript strengthening JavaScript / TypeScript basics for automation writing clean and maintainable automation code locators, assertions, waits, debugging basic framework thinking / structured test design API testing basics Git and GitHub Linux fundamentals relevant to automation work practical use of AI tools for learning, debugging, and improving automation work Preferred format 1:1 sessions only around 3 sessions per week practical learning with live coding / pair programming where useful homework or exercises after sessions review of homework / code in follow-up sessions I want the mentoring to be structured but flexible, depending on my pace and progress. If the first month goes well, I’d be happy to continue into more advanced topics later. What I’m looking for in a mentor practical, hands-on teaching real implementation, not just theory code review and constructive feedback ability to explain concepts clearly guidance on how to think like an Automation Engineer / SDET support with debugging and problem-solving someone who can also help with consistency and accountability What I do not want slide-based teaching generic course-style sessions copy-paste tutorials mostly theoretical explanations without hands-on work a rigid one-size-fits-all program My intention is not just to complete exercises, but to build a clean and well-structured automation project that I can present in my GitHub and also explain in interviews — including design decisions, framework structure, and testing approach. Please include the following in your proposal Your current role Your years of experience in automation Your experience with Playwright and TypeScript Whether you have built a Playwright framework from scratch Your experience with API testing, GitHub, Linux, CI/CD, and cloud basics Whether you have mentored someone from a manual QA / testing background into automation How you would structure the first month for someone like me Whether you provide homework, code review, and feedback A short description of a Playwright framework or automation project you personally designed and built Whether you can support later with interview preparation and portfolio guidance if we continue Engagement approach I would like to begin with a smaller initial package for the first month so that we can assess fit, pace, and teaching style. If the collaboration goes well, I am open to extending it further into more advanced project-based mentoring. Please also include: your availability your rates your preferred session format whether you can support with practical feedback between sessions Thank you.

title: Urgent Children's Book Illustrator. 32-Page Book (Character Designs & Storyboard Already Complete)
desc: I am seeking a professional children's book illustrator to finalize a 32-page picture book. The story follows two young brothers on a mission to ensure their Granny's birthday party is completely gluten-free and safe. The tone is educational, heartwarming, and adventurous. You will not be starting from scratch. I will provide a comprehensive asset package on Day 1. This includes a completed 16-Panel storyboard detailing the exact narrative flow, page spreads, and initial compositions. I'll provide fully established character reference keys and high-quality reference illustrations showing the exact designs, expressions, and clothing for the 4 characters. Style Target: A cozy, warm, and expressive digital watercolor or clean digital style with beautiful linework that perfectly matches our established character models. You will have the full manuscript, text layout preferences, and visual keys from the very beginning. When you submit a milestone, I provide fast, consolidated, actionable bullet-point feedback so you can make quick adjustments without endless revision loops. Respect for technical layouts: Because this book will be published on Amazon KDP, I place a high priority on technical layout integrity. Text placement must be fluidly intertwined within the scenes (think The Gruffalo style), leaving clean, light "quiet spaces" for the text while strictly respecting center gutter and print safety margins. Zero-Tolerance for AI: No generative AI workflows or asset-flipping tools are permitted. Project Timeline & Milestones (3-Week Sprint). The total project duration is 3 weeks (21 days) from the contract start date. The budget will be funded upfront into Upwork Escrow and released across 4 strict sequential milestones: Milestone 1: Progress Batch 1 (Pages 1–8) | Due: Day 5 Deliverable: The first 8 pages fully illustrated and colored to verify style alignment, text placement fluidly integrated into the backgrounds, and correct character age proportions. Payout: 25% of total budget. Milestone 2: Progress Batch 2 (Pages 9–16) | Due: Day 10 Deliverable: The next 8 pages fully illustrated, ensuring perfect character consistency across scenes. Payout: 25% of total budget. Milestone 3: Progress Batch 3 (Pages 17–24) | Due: Day 15 Deliverable: Complete illustrations for pages 17 to 24. Payout: 25% of total budget. Milestone 4: Final Batch, Cover & Handovers (Pages 25–32) | Due: Day 21 Deliverable: The final illustration batch, front/back cover formatting, final technical verification of KDP print margins, and delivery of high-resolution, print-ready files (layered source files + PDF). Payout: 25% of total budget. Requirements to Apply A link to your portfolio demonstrating consistent children's book character rendering (specifically maintaining young child proportions). Confirmation that you can begin immediately and comfortably commit to this 3-week milestone schedule.A brief note on your preferred software (Procreate, Photoshop, etc.). Technical Specifications & Deliverables for Amazon KDP To ensure a seamless upload to Amazon KDP, the final deliverables must strictly adhere to the following publishing standards: Color Space: All files must be built and delivered in CMYK color mode for crisp, accurate physical printing (not RGB). Resolution: Minimum 300 DPI for all final artwork. Final Deliverables Required: Interior File: One single, completely compiled, print-ready PDF containing all 32 interior pages in sequential order, with text embedded and bleed margins correctly applied. Cover File: One single, flattened, print-ready PDF wrap (Back Cover + Spine + Front Cover combined into a single spread) designed precisely to Amazon KDP's cover template dimensions based on the final page count. Source Files: Fully layered source files (layered PSD, Procreate, or AI files) with all text layers left editable so I can make minor textual adjustments in the future if needed.

title: LOGO Creation for Woman Owned Communications & Strategy Co.
desc: Brand & logo designer needed — complete visual identity for a boutique advisory studio I'm looking for an experienced brand/logo designer to create the full visual identity for Alaniz & Co., a female-owned strategic communications, marketing, and advisory studio operating as "An Alaniz Advisors Company." Our clients are founders, C-suite executives, and the businesses they build. The register is discreet luxury and old-money restraint — heritage advisory, not corporate consultancy. Think the stationery of a private bank or the masthead of a fine magazine, not a typical agency site. Typography does most of the work; the mark is minimal and refined. Restraint is the brand. We already have a clear, locked direction. I need a designer to execute and refine it to production quality and deliver a complete system: LOCKED DIRECTION • Name: Alaniz & Co. · Logo mark: "A&CO," with the ampersand (&) as the standalone icon · Wordmark: ALANIZ & CO. • Tagline: Creative instinct. Strategic discipline. • Palette: Black #0B0B0B; Antique Gold #A98643 (accent only — a hairline or foil edge, max 5% of any composition, never a fill or background); Silver #C0C3C7; Charcoal #1C1C1C; Soft White #F3F4F6 • Type: Cinzel (display/wordmark), Montserrat (supporting). Expect custom letter-spacing and kerning work. • Gold runs as a hairline on light backgrounds and as gold foil on dark backgrounds. LOGO LOCKUPS (each as editable vector) 1. Full lockup — mark + ALANIZ & CO. wordmark + "Sandra Y. Alaniz, Principal" 2. Mark only 3. Monochrome — single-color black 4. Reverse on dark — gold foil retained 5. Submark / favicon — the ampersand, must hold at 16px FULL BRANDING • Brand guidelines PDF (logo usage, clear space, minimum size, misuse, color, typography) • Business card — front and back, print-ready • Letterhead and email signature • Social avatar / profile assets FILE FORMATS Editable vector source (AI and SVG), PNG exports at 1x and 2x, and guidelines as PDF. TO APPLY Please share a portfolio of elevated, minimal, or luxury/editorial brand identities. Experience with refined wordmark/monogram design, typographic kerning, and print/foil specs is a strong plus.

title: Visual Identity Designer for Wellbeing Startup
desc: What we need We are commissioning the creative core of our visual identity. We build the full brand manual and all applications in-house, so this engagement is focused. You deliver: - A primary wordmark - A compact brand mark (an abstract icon or monogram that works square, for app icon, favicon, and social avatar) - A defined lockup (wordmark and brand mark combined) - Variants: monochrome and dark mode - An imagery concept with two to three worked examples - A colour system recommendation that supports product-level colour coding - A typeface recommendation (display and body) with multilingual glyph coverage - Core usage specs (clear space, minimum size, monochrome) Constraints that matter to us: - Wordmark-led, with a compact brand mark for small and square formats. The mark must be abstract and must not hint at any single product's subject, because it carries the whole product family. - A master brand with colour-coded product lines (we plan a family of products on the same method). - Discretion. Our imagery never relies on identifiable people, stock couples, or before-after staging. - The brand should not look like generic wellness (no soft pastels, watercolour gradients, ornate scripts) and not clinical. - Works across languages: German, English, Italian, French now, Polish and Nordics later. Full diacritics required. - Built to still hold in five years, not tied to a current trend What we expect from you - A portfolio of brand identity systems, not just single logos. We want to see consistent visual language across a whole identity. - Comfort with typography-led, restrained, adult brands. - Clear, structured communication and a transparent fixed-price proposal by milestone. Screening questions - Share two or three brand identity projects, full systems rather than single logos, that are closest to a calm, adult, typography-led brand. . How would you approach expressing a "body-based" concept visually without showing identifiable people? A short paragraph is fine. - Have you built identities that work across several languages, including full diacritics (for example Polish)? Briefly, how do you handle font selection for that? - Where do you get inspiration from? - Give a fixed-price proposal for the scope above, broken into the three milestones. On full payment of the final milestone, all rights to the delivered identity (wordmark, brand mark, lockup, variants) transfer to us, including worldwide, exclusive, and unrestricted use. Deliverables include the editable source files (e.g. Illustrator, Figma), not only exported formats. Any paid typeface used in the logo must be either converted to outlines or accompanied by a clear note on the licence we need. Please confirm in your proposal that you can transfer full ownership and source files on completion. We will share a detailed brief and full context with shortlisted candidates.

title: Edit a 2-minute vertical (9:16) product video from a ready-made storyboard

It's laid out so you can literally go in order, scene by scene. All on-screen images as full-resolution PNGs (already designed and branded), including the title card and end card. The voiceover (I record it separately and send you the audio). Brand colors and font. Your job is to assemble it cleanly with simple motion, a music bed, and burned-in captions. No glitchy or sci-fi effects. Smooth zooms and fades. One custom animation (the centerpiece): Most scenes are just gentle zooms/fades on the images I provide. There is one hero sequence where a small "capsule" icon flies from one AI logo to the next (six logos) and each one "rebuilds" the same card. Please confirm you're comfortable building this. Voiceover note: I record the VO myself and send it to you. Cut the video to match the VO timing and keep the music low underneath. If the audio isn't ready when you start, build to the written script (included) and I'll drop it in. Timeline: 3–5 days. One round of revisions included. To apply, please: Share 1–2 examples of short-form / vertical motion-graphic videos you've made Confirm you can build the logo-to-logo "capsule" animation described above Tell me your estimated delivery time I'll send the full storyboard and all assets on award.

title: Odoo.sh Developer: Fix Product Catalog UI/Logic to Allow Duplicate Products in Multiple Sections
desc: We are looking for an experienced Odoo Developer to fix an edge case in our sales quotation flow on Odoo.sh (Custom Plan). The Issue: We customized our quotation view by removing the standard "Add a Product" inline link and forcing users to use the "Catalog" button instead (so they can view product images while ordering). However, our clients require quotes broken down by rooms/sections. Currently, if a user adds "Product A" (e.g., 4 quantities) under Section 1 using the Catalog, and then tries to add "Product A" again under Section 2 via the Catalog, Odoo remembers the previous selection. Instead of creating a new line item under the new section, it just points back to the first line or updates its quantity. What We Need: Modify the Odoo Catalog UI/UX logic (OWL/JavaScript & Python backend) so that it allows users to add the same product multiple times as separate line items under different section lines. Ensure the catalog resets or differentiates selections based on the active section the user is currently under. Requirements: Proven experience with Odoo backend (Python) and frontend (OWL/JS framework) customization. Experience working with Odoo.sh environments and git workflows. Strong understanding of the sale.order and sale.order.line models and the standard Catalog wizard functionality. Please reply with your experience handling Odoo Catalog/Wizard customizations and your estimated timeline for this fix.

title: Illustrator for Wedding Stationery & Themes
desc: We are building a premium wedding-planning platform. Everything a couple makes inside the app (their wedding website, their printed invitations, their save-the-dates, their guest emails) shares one visual theme, so it all feels like one designed whole. The themes are the heart of the product, and the illustration quality is what will make or break it. We need an illustrator (or a small team) to create the illustration assets for 12 distinct wedding themes. To be clear about what we are NOT asking: we do not want finished, fixed invitation cards. We have built our own rendering engine that places text and lays out the design. What we need from you is the raw illustration elements that our engine arranges, so the same theme can flow responsively across a tall invitation, a wide website header, a square format, and an email. Think of it the way Canva works: you create a library of beautiful elements, and our software composes them. That separation is essential. What we need per theme A set of LOOSE, individual illustration elements (not a single locked composition): 2 to 4 corner ornaments (e.g. top-left, bottom-right) 1 full border / wreath arrangement 3 to 5 individual sprigs, branches or floral stems that can be placed freely 1 to 2 divider / flourish line elements Any small accent marks that fit the theme Plus a short style sheet per theme: the recommended display font, body font and script/accent font (Google Fonts or clearly licensed), and the color palette (hex values). We already have draft palettes and can share them, you refine and confirm. The 12 themes span a wide stylistic range, including (examples, final list shared on start): airy botanical watercolor, fine pen-line florals, minimalist sage with large serif type, art-deco geometric, wildflower meadow, romantic peony, celestial / night, and a hand-drawn playful style.
