What it is: A business development system for small business owners and professional services firms who want consistent pipeline without burning hours on BD every week. Three focused hours per week, done in a specific rhythm, compounds over time. Audience: Small business owners, consultants, fractional executives, and sales professionals — primarily in professional services. Educated, busy, skeptical of hustle-culture marketing. Tone: Clean, credible, professional. Think established consultant, not startup casual. Trustworthy and direct. Not corporate-stiff, but not bro-y either. Closest visual references: Basecamp, Notion, a boutique management consulting firm. Colors | Blue | `#4A6FA5` | Primary — CTA buttons, icon accent, links | | Blue Light | `#5A82B8` | Hover states only | | Blue Subtle | `#EDF1F7` | Backgrounds, callout cards | | Black | `#111111` | Logo wordmark, primary headings | | Dark | `#1A1A1A` | Dark backgrounds | | White | `#FFFFFF` | Default page background | | Gray 700 | `#444444` | Body text | | Gray 500 | `#777777` | Supporting text | | Gray 300 | `#BBBBBB` | Borders | | Gray 100 | `#F5F5F5` | Alternate section backgrounds | ## Typography - **Headings:** Playfair Display (Google Fonts, free) - **Body / UI:** Inter (Google Fonts, free) ## What I Need You to Design 1. Logo System A logo with two components: an icon mark and a wordmark. Deliver both together and the icon alone as a standalone mark. 2. Logo Variations Deliver all of the following: - Full logo (icon + wordmark) on white background - Full logo (icon + wordmark) on dark (#1A1A1A) background - Icon mark only (standalone) on white - Icon mark only on dark - Horizontal and stacked versions if both are viable - One-color version in blue (#4A6FA5) on white 3. Brand Style Sheet (one page) A clean one-page PDF showing: - Logo usage and clear space rules - Color palette with hex codes - Typography pairing (Playfair Display + Inter) - Do/don't usage examples What I Am Providing - Complete HTML landing page as the primary visual reference (shared after hiring) - All copy - Brand colors and fonts as specified above - Feedback within 24 hours on all rounds I might need a website if you're open to add that later. Do not use: briefcases, handshakes, bar charts, lightbulbs, generic arrows, LinkedIn-style icons, or anything that reads as generic "business" clipart. No drop shadows. No gradients. No decorative script fonts. Questions to Answer in Your Proposal Share two or three logo or brand identity projects from your portfolio, ideally for service businesses or programs (not product companies or consumer brands). How many initial concepts do you present in the first round? Have you designed for a personal brand or thought leadership product before?

title: Team Logo Illustration
desc: I need to optimize this image. It's supposed to be a collage of the hometown of people on my team with all the places that they mentioned. Please optimize it so no weird artifacts, each country has one flag, its seamless and no weird intersection of countries, etc. Do your best! Example attached. Example Prompt attached: Create a polished **hand-drawn doodle art team emblem** for a team named **“Catalog Quality.”** The design should look like a colorful collaborative doodle mural transformed into a clean badge or sticker. Use **bold black hand-drawn outlines, simple iconic shapes, playful sketch details, small decorative marks, arrows, swirls, dots, stars, motion lines, and sticker-like doodle elements**. The style should feel whimsical, energetic, and handmade, like a high-quality illustrated notebook doodle or creative team whiteboard drawing. The words **“Catalog Quality”** must be centered prominently as the main focal point. Make the lettering bold, clean, readable, and hand-drawn, like custom doodle lettering. Keep the text unobstructed. Arrange all icons around the centered text in a balanced circular or badge-like composition. The landmarks and symbols should wrap naturally around the team name, forming one cohesive global collage. Do not create separate realistic scenes. Each place should be simplified into a recognizable doodle icon. Use vibrant but harmonious colors, clean spacing, and a playful team-logo aesthetic. The final image should be dense and rich, but still readable. Include a small, clearly recognizable **flag doodle for every country represented** somewhere in the composition: **Mexico, Brazil, Canada, United States, India, Nigeria, Chile, China, Malaysia, and Hong Kong**. The flags should be integrated naturally as small waving flags, sticker badges, banners, pins, or decorative pennants near the related landmarks. Also include the **Maryland state flag** near the Washington, DC / Maryland section. Keep all flags simplified but identifiable. Include these doodle icons: **Mexico** Aztec calendar, Angel of Independence, Xochimilco chinampa with flowers and canals, plus a small Mexican flag. **Belo Horizonte, Brazil** Igreja da Pampulha with its curved modernist church shape, plus a small Brazilian flag. **Maranhão, Brazil** Bumba Meu Boi, shown as a spectacular festive mythical ox with decorative patterns, ribbons, embroidery-like details, music, dance, and theatrical folk-performance energy. It should feel rooted in Maranhão’s June festivals, like a vibrant street celebration in São Luís, blending music, dance, theater, and visual art around the traditional mythical ox story of death and resurrection. Add a small Brazilian flag nearby. **Brazil — Cordel / Xilogravura Style Accent** Include a small inset or decorative sticker element inspired by **xilogravura de cordel**, the traditional Brazilian folk-literature woodcut style. Make it rustic, high-contrast, bold, and graphic, like a simplified black-and-white or limited-color woodcut print that captures the beauty of regional storytelling and festival imagery. This should be a style accent within the doodle badge, not the entire image style. **Lençóis Maranhenses, Brazil** Simplify Lençóis Maranhenses National Park into a doodle icon of sweeping white sand dunes with turquoise-blue rainwater lagoons, using curved lines and playful color blocks. **Canada** Maple leaves, CN Tower, Banff mountain peaks, Vancouver Canucks-inspired orca breaching through a stylized “C,” green Lions Gate Bridge with Stanley Park greenery and North Shore mountains, Stanley Park Totem Poles, and the pixelated Digital Orca sculpture, plus a small Canadian flag. **Ottawa, Canada** A winter skating scene on the Rideau Canal and the Canadian Parliament Building, simplified as cheerful doodle icons, with the Canadian flag visible near the Parliament Building. **Atlanta, USA** One Atlantic Center “pencil building,” Mercedes-Benz Stadium, and simplified Atlanta downtown skyline, plus a small United States flag. **Princeton, USA** Ivy-covered Princeton University-style collegiate building and Princeton Junction Fire Department, plus a small United States flag. **Los Angeles, USA** Hollywood Sign on green-brown hills, with the word **“HOLLYWOOD”** clearly readable, plus a small United States flag. **Washington, DC / Maryland, USA** Lincoln Memorial, Reflecting Pool, a blue crab, a small United States flag, and the Maryland state flag with its distinctive red, white, black, and gold pattern. **Delhi, India** India Gate, shown as a prominent national monument and war memorial in the heart of New Delhi, simplified into a recognizable doodle landmark, plus a small Indian flag. **Nigeria** A proud eagle representing Nigeria’s soccer team mascot and the Lekki-Ikoyi Bridge, simplified as bold, energetic doodle icons, plus a small Nigerian flag. **Chile** Cerro San Cristóbal in Santiago with the white Virgin Mary statue, city skyline, and the **Andes Mountains** behind it. Add snowy Santiago apartment-and-office-view mountains. Include Valparaíso funicular elevators on colorful steep hills, Torres del Paine granite towers with turquoise lake, El Tatio geysers with desert steam, Easter Island moai statues, Colchagua vineyard grapevines, Atacama Desert observatory under a Milky Way sky, Chillán ski center with snowy slopes, and a completo italiano hot dog with tomato, avocado, and mayonnaise, plus a small Chilean flag. **Xi’an, China** Terracotta Army soldiers, Bell Tower, Drum Tower, Xi’an city walls, Giant Wild Goose Pagoda, and Tang Dynasty Ever Bright City lights, plus a small Chinese flag. **Shenzhen, China** A spectacular drone light show above the Shenzhen Civic Center in Futian District, with synchronized illuminated skyscrapers, glowing geometric drone formations, and a futuristic skyline, simplified into colorful doodle shapes. Add a small Chinese flag nearby. **Malaysia** Kuala Lumpur Twin Towers as a clean, recognizable doodle landmark, plus a hibiscus flower representing Malaysia’s national flower. Add a small Malaysian flag nearby. **Hong Kong** Victoria Harbour with a simplified skyline, harbor water, and small boats, plus a playful dim sum icon such as bamboo steamers or dumplings. Add a small Hong Kong flag nearby. Composition instructions: Make the overall silhouette feel like a finished doodle badge. Put large landmarks around the outer ring and smaller icons in the gaps. Prioritize immediate recognizability for: CN Tower, Lions Gate Bridge, Hollywood Sign, Cerro San Cristóbal with the Andes Mountains, Valparaíso elevators, Torres del Paine, Easter Island moai, Atacama observatory, completo italiano, Angel of Independence, Terracotta soldiers, Mercedes-Benz Stadium, Lincoln Memorial, Digital Orca, India Gate, Canadian Parliament Building, Rideau Canal skating, Lekki-Ikoyi Bridge, Nigerian eagle mascot, Bumba Meu Boi, Lençóis Maranhenses dunes and lagoons, Shenzhen Civic Center light show, KL Twin Towers, hibiscus flower, Victoria Harbour, dim sum, the country flags, and the Maryland flag. Use doodle-style simplification: expressive outlines, flat colors, minimal shading, hand-drawn texture, playful proportions, and small decorative filler elements. Keep every object illustrated, not photographic. Avoid photorealism, 3D rendering, realistic lighting, heavy shadows, cluttered overlapping, excessive tiny detail, messy text, distorted lettering, extra words, watermarks, and blank unused space. The final result should be a vibrant, charming, hand-drawn doodle-art global team logo celebrating city pride, collaboration, and quality.

title: Product Designer + CAD Designer Needed for Premium Baseball-Shaped Trading Card Vault
desc: OVERVIEW We are developing a premium collectible sports product called the Baseball Vault. The Baseball Vault is a baseball-shaped collectible storage product designed to hold and protect trading cards. It combines sports memorabilia, premium collectible design, and practical card storage. We are looking for a designer who can help transform an early prototype concept into a polished, premium, manufacturable product. The ideal partner can support both: - Industrial / Product Design - CAD Development We are especially interested in someone who can improve the concept, not simply execute a predetermined design. ⸻ DESIGN FIRST, CAD SECOND We do not want to immediately jump into final CAD. The first phase of the project is design exploration. We want to review and approve the overall product direction before moving into detailed CAD development. We are looking for a designer who can contribute ideas, identify opportunities, challenge assumptions, and elevate the product experience. ⸻ PRODUCT CONCEPT The Baseball Vault is: - Shaped like a baseball - Designed to store trading cards (not baseballs) - Built as a two-piece shell - Intended to close with magnets - Designed with a clean opening experience - Intended to include a soft felt or felt-like interior - Potentially designed with interchangeable inserts for different card formats Potential card formats include: - Raw cards - Sleeved cards - Top loaders - Graded/slabbed cards The product should feel like: - A premium sports keepsake - A collectible object - A giftable product - A polished consumer product The product should not feel like: - A cheap plastic box - A generic 3D print - A novelty item - A basic storage container ⸻ BRAND CONCEPT We are building a premium sports-inspired consumer product brand. Our design direction is: - Premium but approachable - Collectible but functional - Sports-inspired without feeling like cheap fan merchandise - Modern - Tactile - Giftable - Display-worthy This product may become the first product in a broader product line and should help establish a recognizable design language and quality standard. We are not looking for a one-off novelty product. We want something that feels like the beginning of a premium consumer brand. Additional brand context can be shared with the selected designer if needed. ⸻ KEY DESIGN CHALLENGES 1. Premium Baseball Exterior The product should immediately read as a baseball while feeling refined, collectible, and premium. Areas to explore: - Baseball stitching interpretation - Seam placement - Leather-inspired surface treatments - Matte or tactile finishes - Sports memorabilia inspiration - Modern industrial design influences We are interested in solutions that evoke baseball leather without requiring actual leather materials. 2. Opening & Closure Experience The vault should feel satisfying and intentional to open and close. Areas to explore: - Magnetic closure - Hidden magnet integration - Finger/thumb access - Alignment systems - Closure feel - Tactile experience The opening experience should feel premium rather than purely functional. 3. Interior Experience The interior should feel protective and elevated. Areas to explore: - Felt or felt-like lining - Card presentation - Card accessibility - Interchangeable inserts - Support for multiple card formats - Premium presentation when opened We are interested in making the interior feel thoughtful and intentional rather than a simple storage cavity. 4. Manufacturability Initial production will likely utilize high-quality 3D printing. Future manufacturing methods may include: - FDM - MJF/SLS - Resin - Injection molding The design should consider: - Reducing support requirements - Reducing visible support scars - Minimizing post-processing labor - Improving print efficiency - Improving assembly efficiency - Scalability toward future manufacturing methods We are not looking for a design that only looks good in renders. It should be practical to prototype, print, assemble, and eventually manufacture. 5. Size & Efficiency The product should be as compact as reasonably possible while still supporting the intended card formats and maintaining a premium experience. Areas to consider: - Minimizing overall size - Minimizing material usage - Maximizing usable interior space - Comfortable handling - Efficient shipping footprint The goal is to create a product that feels intentional and efficient without feeling undersized. 6. Assembly The product may include: - Top shell - Bottom shell - Connector or hinge component - Magnets - Felt insert - Internal card insert The design should make assembly as simple and repeatable as possible. Areas to explore: - Self-aligning components - Magnet pockets - Felt installation - Insert installation - Reduced glue dependency - Assembly efficiency 7. Product Presentation & Unboxing We would like the designer to consider the overall customer experience, not just the object itself. Areas to explore: - Product presentation - Packaging concepts - Gift-worthy experience - Collector-oriented presentation - Branded insert concepts - Ways to make the product feel memorable and premium We are not necessarily looking for final packaging artwork, but we do want the product design to consider presentation and unboxing. 8. Design for Cost While the product should feel premium, we are also interested in solutions that support healthy manufacturing economics. Areas to consider: - Material efficiency - Print efficiency - Assembly efficiency - Packaging efficiency - Scalability We are interested in thoughtful tradeoffs that improve product viability without compromising the overall customer experience. ⸻ CORE DESIGN PHILOSOPHY The goal is not to maximize complexity. The goal is to maximize: - Perceived quality - Collectibility - Customer delight - Giftability - Manufacturability while keeping production and assembly as simple as possible. ⸻ PHASE 1 DELIVERABLE– DESIGN EXPLORATION Please propose approximately 2–3 design directions. Deliverables may include: - Concept sketches - Product renderings - Exterior concepts - Interior concepts - Opening/closure concepts - Material recommendations - Manufacturing recommendations - Packaging/presentation concepts - Suggestions for improving the overall product We want to approve the design direction before CAD development begins. ⸻ PHASE 2 DELIVERABLES - CAD DEVELOPMENT After design approval: - Production-ready CAD - STEP files - STL files - Organized source files - Separate printable/manufacturable components - Manufacturing recommendations - Print orientation recommendations where appropriate The CAD should be suitable for prototyping and future refinement. ⸻ IDEAL EXPERIENCE Strong candidates may have experience with: - Industrial design - Consumer products - Collectibles - Toys - Premium gift products - Sports products - Desk accessories - Design for manufacturing - CAD for physical products - 3D printed products Experience with magnets, hinges, packaging, collectibles, MJF/SLS, injection molding, or premium consumer products is a plus.

title: Fitness App Launch & Growth Strategist Needed for Influencer-Led App
desc: We are launching a new fitness app. The app is already close to launch. We are using a white-label app solution, and the app is expected to go live soon on the App Store and Google Play. We are not looking for an app developer at this stage. We are looking for an experienced Fitness App Launch & Growth Strategist who can help us successfully launch, activate, monetize, and retain users inside the app. Our team already has: -A positioned personal brand in the fitness space -An existing audience waiting for the app -Content and marketing experience -A white-label fitness app almost ready -A team that can execute content, community, and marketing What we need is someone who has real experience launching and growing fitness, wellness, coaching, habit, or subscription-based mobile apps. What we need help with We need a launch and growth expert to help us with: 1. Pre-launch readiness Review the app experience before launch Identify gaps in onboarding, activation, pricing, and user journey Help us define what must be ready before going public Create a launch checklist for App Store and Google Play Review app positioning, screenshots, descriptions, and store messaging 2. Launch strategy Build a clear launch plan for the first 30–60 days Help us convert Andreana Fitness’ audience into app users Design the launch campaign using Instagram, TikTok, YouTube, email, WhatsApp, and community Create a waitlist / early access / founding members strategy if needed Recommend how to structure the first offer 3. Monetization Recommend subscription structure Help define pricing, free trial, introductory offer, or founding member offer Review paywall and conversion flow Suggest upsell or retention opportunities 4. Activation and retention Improve the first-time user experience Define the first 7-day and 30-day user journey Recommend push notification strategy Recommend challenges, progress tracking, habit loops, community, and accountability mechanics Help reduce drop-off after download 5. Metrics and analytics Define the most important metrics to track from day one Help us understand what events we should measure Create a simple dashboard structure for the team Recommend weekly review process after launch 6. Team guidance Work with our Spanish-speaking team Help us translate strategy into clear execution tasks Guide our content, marketing, support, and app teams during launch Help us avoid common mistakes in fitness app launches Ideal candidate You should have experience with at least one of the following: Fitness apps Wellness apps Coaching apps Habit tracking apps Nutrition apps Subscription-based mobile apps Influencer-led app launches Mobile app growth App Store Optimization App onboarding and retention Consumer subscription products Spanish is strongly preferred. Our team communicates best in Spanish, although we can understand English. The ideal candidate should be comfortable working with a Spanish-speaking team. Deliverables For the first phase, we expect: App launch audit Pre-launch checklist 30–60 day launch strategy Monetization and pricing recommendations Onboarding and retention recommendations App Store / Google Play optimization recommendations Metrics and analytics plan Weekly action plan for the team Engagement We are open to: A fixed-price launch strategy sprint An hourly consulting engagement A short-term advisory role during the launch This could become a longer-term role if the launch goes well.

title: Food Technologist Needed for Confectionery Coating Process
desc: We are seeking an experienced food technologist with expertise in confectionery to help us develop a method for coating individual hazelnuts with a thin layer of pelamushi, a mixture of boiled grape juice and flour. The ideal candidate will have a strong background in food science and technology, particularly in coatings and confections, drying systems, anti sticking solutions. I am developing a modern snack product inspired by traditional Georgian churchkhela, redesigned into individually coated nuts suitable for loose packaging. Unlike traditional churchkhela (nuts threaded on a string), the goal is to produce separate coated nuts that can be packaged loose without sticking to each other or absorbing excess moisture. .Each product unit consists of a single hazelnut, coated with a thin, smooth fruit-based layer (initially grape-based pelamushi), forming a uniform, round coating around each nut. The finished product will be packaged loose, with approximately 30-50 coated hazelnuts per pouch, including mixed fruit flavors maybe artificial flavouring will be also needed and natural colors (derived from different fruit juices) Key Requirements & Challenges: . Coating must be: ◦ thin, smooth, and visually uniform o non-sticky even when multiple pieces are in contact ○ stable inside sealed packaging . Initial variants: ◦ Grape juice (sugar-free) ◦ Future fruit variants (apple, pomegranate, berry blends), some may require added sugar . Clean-label approach preferred • No moisture migration or surface sweating . Shelf-stable at room *-temperature. expected Outcome: • Optimized formulation for grape-based coating . Recommended coating and drying process . Guidance on anti-sticking strategy . Packaging considerations for moisture control . Feasibility assessment for scale-up. Collaboration Format: . R&D consultation . Recipe/formulation development . Process design guidance (Remote collaboration is acceptable)

title: AI-Generated Graphics Specialist
desc: **AI-Generated Graphics Specialist — Amazon A+ Content & Listing Images (Health/OTC Supplements)** --- **About Us** We run two growing OTC medicine and supplement brands selling across Amazon, Walmart, eBay, TikTok Shop, and Shopify DTC. Catalog includes sleep aids, sinus/pain relief, gummies, and softgels. Typical turnaround and price for a full A+ + listing image package **No relevant AI portfolio = not reviewed.**

title: Senior Full-Stack Developer Needed for a LinkedIn-like Professional Platform
desc: # Senior Full-Stack Developer Needed for a LinkedIn-like Professional Platform **C#/.NET, Next.js/React, PostgreSQL, AWS** We are looking for an experienced senior full-stack developer or a small senior team to build a LinkedIn-like professional platform. This will be a milestone-based project. We will start with the first milestone only. If the first milestone is delivered with good quality, clean code, clear communication, and a solid technical foundation, we will continue with the remaining scope. ## Preferred Tech Stack * Backend: C# / .NET * Frontend: Next.js, React, or a similar modern frontend framework * Database: PostgreSQL on Amazon RDS * Cloud: AWS * Object/file storage: AWS S3 or another suitable AWS-native solution * Deployment: AWS-based production deployment ## Timeline The first milestone should be completed in **1 week**. The client target for the full project is **June 25**. This may be aggressive, so please be honest in your proposal. If you think the timeline is not realistic, clearly explain what can realistically be delivered by that date. ## Project Summary The product is a professional networking and company experience platform. It includes individual users, company accounts, user/company profiles, content sharing, professional experience/review flows, job-related flows, interview-related flows, CV/portfolio features, academy/career content, messaging/networking features, and an admin panel. The exact business details will be shared later if needed. For now, the scope below should be enough for an experienced developer to estimate the project and propose a technical solution. --- # Milestone 1 — Architecture Foundation, Registration, and Profiles **Target duration: 1 week** This is the trial milestone. ## Scope ### Technical Foundation * Create backend project structure * Create frontend project structure * Define initial architecture and module boundaries * Set up PostgreSQL database foundation * Prepare initial database schema and migrations * Set up environment configuration * Add basic logging/error-handling structure * Prepare local development setup * Prepare initial AWS deployment approach or basic deployment ### Authentication and Authorization * Individual user registration * Company registration * Login/logout flow * Password reset foundation * Role foundation: * Individual user * Company user * Admin * Secure authentication/authorization approach should be proposed and implemented in a basic form ### User Profile * Create and edit individual profile * Basic profile information: * Name * Title/profession * City/location * Sector * Contact preferences * Short bio * Profile photo upload foundation * Profile completion percentage foundation * Public profile view foundation * Open/anonymous identity concept foundation ### Company Profile * Create and edit company profile * Company registration data * Company contact data * Company verification foundation * Company profile page foundation * Company logo/image upload foundation ### Expected Deliverables * Working frontend and backend * User registration/login * Company registration/login * User profile create/edit * Company profile create/edit * PostgreSQL schema foundation * Initial tests * Initial deployment plan * Short documentation: * How to run locally * How to deploy * Architecture overview * What is included/excluded in Milestone 1 After this milestone, the code quality, architecture, maintainability, and communication will be reviewed before continuing. --- # Milestone 2 — Core User Platform ## Scope ### Home / Feed * Main logged-in homepage * Feed layout * Create post * Create professional experience/review post * Anonymous or public sharing option * Image attachment foundation * Poll creation foundation * Feed categories/tabs * Like/useful action * Commenting * Replying to comments * Save/bookmark content * Share/copy link actions * Report content flow * Follow user/company * Add user to network ### Search * Global search page * Search by: * Companies * People * Content * Topics * Search result tabs * Basic search filters * Empty state and loading state ### Professional Experiences / Reviews * Experience listing page * Experience detail foundation * Write experience flow * Multi-step experience submission: * Experience details * Company selection * Supporting document upload foundation * Final publish/submit step * Anonymous/public experience option * Experience status tracking: * Published title: Art Editing Assistant — Photoshop, Topaz Photo AI & ON1 Resize | Ongoing Work
desc: We are seeking a reliable, detail-oriented editor to prepare original digital artwork for large-format print production and e-commerce. All work follows a comprehensive, well-documented SOP. The right candidate is disciplined enough to follow a defined process exactly, and experienced enough to know when to pause, ask a question, or flag something before moving forward. Editors who communicate proactively and take ownership of their work are a great fit here. A full SOP is provided before work begins. For candidates who prefer it, we are also happy to do a brief orientation call to get acquainted, answer any questions, and make sure everything is clear before the first task. The Work: This role has two stages, each with its own rate. Stage 1 — Editing Pipeline: $2 per artwork Each artwork goes through a full editing pipeline: AI upscaling in Topaz Photo AI, set image reconstruction in Photoshop, and final export in multiple formats. The $2 rate covers the complete pipeline for one artwork — from raw original through all final exported files. Exporting multiple file formats from one artwork does not count as multiple artworks. The reconstruction stage is the most involved part of the work. It requires identifying how the original artwork was composed, extracting the correct section, and rebuilding a clean symmetrical composition using the upscaled original as source material. Precise alignment is essential. Each image also requires embedded text that blends seamlessly with the artwork. Stage 2 — Mockup Creation: $12 per artwork Once an artwork is fully exported, mockups are created across 8 merchandise products and 13 wall art products using Printify, Prodigi, Canvy, and Photoshop. The $12 rate covers all mockups for one artwork. Some products are straightforward — upload the file and save the mockup. Others require creative judgment, such as selecting room colors and compositions in Canvy, or editing PSD templates for cushion and mug set products. All files are stored and shared via Box. Editors will need a Box account to access source files and submit completed work. Trial Structure: Editing trial: 4 artworks ($8) — editing pipeline only Mockup trial: 1 artwork ($12) — follows once editing trial is approved First milestone after trial approval: to be determined Ongoing: Many artworks are available for verified editors Payment: Released upon quality-approved delivery Active Editing Time: Editing pipeline: approximately 12 minutes per artwork, not including render and loading time. Mockup creation: approximately 1.5 hours per artwork across all 21 products. System Requirements: All requirements are mandatory. Please do not apply if your system does not meet the minimum specifications. RAM: 32 GB minimum (64 GB preferred) GPU: Dedicated graphics card with 4 GB+ VRAM Storage: SSD required (NVMe preferred) Software: Photoshop, Topaz Photo AI, and ON1 Resize OS: Windows 10/11 or macOS 11+ What We're Looking For: Strong Photoshop skills — layer management, guides, precise alignment, and symmetrical composition Comfortable working with very large files Meticulous with file naming, versioning, and folder organization Professional and communicative — asks questions when something is unclear Able to make creative decisions for mockup room styling and product presentation Trustworthy and discreet with proprietary artwork Experience with print production is a plus but not required Ownership & Confidentiality: All edited files remain the exclusive property of our studio. By accepting this project, the contractor agrees not to share, reuse, reproduce, or distribute any artwork or project files in any form.

title: Flutter + Supabase + Python Developer Needed for AI Pool Tech Mobile App
desc: We are building Pool Pal, an AI Pool Tech Concierge mobile app for homeowners and pool technicians. Pool Pal helps homeowners maintain their pools through guided onboarding, pool profile setup, photo uploads, water test logs, testing kit recommendations, weekly maintenance checklists, pool care lessons, and AI-guided troubleshooting. Estimated project scope: 150–250 hours for the homeowner MVP, depending on final polish, AI depth, Stripe setup, and QA. We want to start with a 10–20 hour paid architecture phase, then move into milestone-based development. We expect this to be vibe coded using Claude as well, the structure, flow and logic have all been designed. The database has been partly created in Supabase. Hand off documents will be provided to the developer. This role will turn into a full time position once development is complete. We are looking for a serious, detail-oriented developer who can help build the MVP using: -Flutter for the mobile frontend -Supabase for authentication, database, storage, and backend services -Python for backend logic, AI workflows, calculations, and integrations -Stripe for subscriptions -OpenAI / ChatGPT API or similar for AI guidance -Photo upload and storage workflows -Role-based access control -Mobile-first UX This is not just a simple app mockup. We need someone who can think through app architecture, database structure, user roles, permissions, paid access gates, backend logic, and scalable MVP foundations. Project Overview Pool Pal will have two main user types: Homeowners and Pool Techs, but for the MVP we will focus only on the homeowners. 1. Homeowners Homeowners should be able to: Create an account Select a membership plan Complete a pool onboarding wizard Create a pool profile Upload pool photos Upload equipment photos Upload test strip photos Log water test readings Receive testing kit recommendations Follow weekly pool maintenance checklists Receive reminders Use AI-guided pool troubleshooting Access basic product and dosage recommendation logic Access a course section with Loom video embeds Download PDFs Complete quizzes or progress tracking See profile completion score View a simple dashboard The app should support these membership levels: Free Homeowner Plan -Limited onboarding -One pool profile -Limited photo uploads -Basic testing kit recommendation - Limited AI guidance Homeowner Pro: Allows for more upload, more guidance, etc. Required Technical Skills: The ideal developer must have strong experience with: -Flutter mobile app development -Dart -Supabase authentication -Supabase Postgres database design -Supabase Storage for photo uploads -Supabase row-level security policies -Python backend development -API design -OpenAI API or similar AI API integration -Image upload and image analysis workflows -Stripe subscriptions -Role-based access control -Push notifications or reminder systems -Clean mobile UI/UX implementation -Scalable database schema design -GitHub / version control -App deployment support Nice-to-Have Skills Experience with any of the following is a plus: -Home service apps -Pool service, maintenance, or field service apps -Health, diagnostic, checklist, or recommendation apps -AI assistants -Flutter + Supabase production apps -App Store and Google Play deployment -Subscription apps -Photo-based AI workflows -Admin dashboards -SaaS MVPs App Architecture Requirements We need the developer to help build and advise on: -Flutter app structure -Supabase database schema -User authentication -Role-based access -Membership access gates -Photo storage -AI request flow -Python backend logic -Water reading calculations -Testing kit recommendation logic -Pool product database -Checklist system -Course access system -Stripe billing integration -Admin dashboard or admin tools -Scalable foundation for future app store launch -Data Models Needed The app will likely need Supabase tables such as: Users Memberships Pools Pool Tech Clients Water Readings Photos Checklists Checklist Items Testing Kit Recommendations Products AI Reports Courses Lessons Lesson Progress Quizzes Notifications Payments / Subscriptions The developer should be comfortable designing and implementing these tables with proper row-level security and access rules. AI Features The app should be built to support AI-powered features such as: -AI pool diagnosis based on readings and photos -AI explanation of what may be wrong with the pool -AI equipment troubleshooting with safety limits -AI test strip photo reading, if feasible -AI product recommendation based on database logic -AI-generated pool health summary -Stored AI report history The AI must follow safety guardrails. The app should not provide unsafe chemical or equipment instructions. The AI should be constrained by: -Pool profile -Water readings -Product database -Safety rules -Escalation rules -Professional-help recommendations when needed -Project Phases Phase 1: Technical Planning and Architecture The developer will: -Review product specs -Confirm MVP scope -Design Supabase schema -Define user roles -Define app navigation -Recommend backend structure -Create a technical build plan -Identify risks and tradeoffs Phase 2: MVP Build -The developer will build: -Flutter UI -Signup/login -Homeowner onboarding -Pool tech onboarding -Pool profile creation -Water readings log -Photo uploads -Weekly checklist -Course section -Membership access gates -Basic user dashboard Phase 3: AI and Logic Integration The developer will implement or prepare: -Testing kit recommendation logic -AI assistant flow -Photo upload context for AI -Product database logic -Pool health summary -Safety guardrails -Escalation rules Phase 4: Subscription, Testing, and Launch Prep readings log -Photo upload system -Checklist system -Testing kit recommendation logic -Course / Loom embed section -Subscription access gates -Stripe subscription integration or setup-ready structure working from detailed product specs -Suggest technical improvements -Be honest about what is feasible -Understand AI API cost and usage limits -Work independently -Provide timelines and milestones title: Senior Mechanical Engineer / PE Needed for MEP HVAC & Plumbing Design Support
desc: MDS Engineering is seeking a Senior Mechanical Engineer / Professional Engineer to support our growing MEP consulting workload on a freelance, 1099, or project-based basis. We are looking for an experienced mechanical engineer who can assist with HVAC design, plumbing design, equipment selection, code review, quality control, and coordination on commercial, institutional, healthcare, government, and infrastructure-related building projects. This role is ideal for a licensed mechanical engineer who has strong consulting engineering experience and can support projects independently while coordinating with our internal team. About MDS Engineering MDS Engineering is a growing minority and woman-owned MEP engineering firm based in York, Pennsylvania. We provide mechanical, electrical, plumbing, fire protection, energy, and building systems design services for clients across multiple sectors, including healthcare, higher education, commercial, government, industrial, and infrastructure projects. We are looking to build a reliable network of experienced freelance engineers who can support MDS on current and future projects. Scope of Work The selected consultant may assist with the following: • HVAC system design and layout • Plumbing system design and coordination • Hydronic systems, chilled water, hot water, steam, and specialty piping design • Domestic water, sanitary, storm, natural gas, and specialty plumbing systems • Mechanical equipment selection and scheduling • Load calculations and engineering calculations • Code review for IMC, IPC, ASHRAE, NFPA, energy code, and local requirements • Review and development of construction documents • Coordination with architectural, electrical, plumbing, and structural disciplines • Review of Revit and AutoCAD drawings • QA/QC review of drawings, specifications, and calculations • Support with construction administration, RFIs, submittals, and field observation reports as needed • General technical support for MEP design projects Ideal Candidate The ideal candidate should have: • 10+ years of mechanical engineering design experience • Active Professional Engineer license preferred • Strong HVAC and plumbing design background • Experience with commercial, institutional, healthcare, government, or industrial projects • Ability to work independently and communicate clearly with project teams • Experience reviewing construction documents and coordinating with other disciplines • Familiarity with Revit and AutoCAD workflows • Strong understanding of applicable mechanical, plumbing, energy, and building codes • Consulting engineering experience preferred • Ability to support projects on an as-needed, deadline-driven basis Preferred Qualifications • PE license in Pennsylvania or surrounding states preferred • Multi-state PE licensure is a plus • Experience with healthcare, higher education, government, transportation, or infrastructure-related projects is a plus • Experience with energy-efficient design, sustainable design, LEED, or building performance analysis is a plus • Ability to provide QA/QC review and redline drawings is strongly preferred Important Notes This opportunity is intended for freelance, 1099, or project-based support. Work will be assigned based on project needs, availability, qualifications, and fit. Professional engineering stamping or sealing services will only be considered where the consultant is properly licensed and where all applicable state requirements for responsible charge, review, and professional practice are met. Please Include in Your Proposal Please provide the following: • Resume or company profile • PE license status and states where licensed • Relevant MEP/HVAC/plumbing project experience • Revit and AutoCAD experience • Typical hourly rate • Weekly availability • Examples of similar work, if available • Confirmation that you are open to ongoing 1099/project-based support Screening Questions Are you a licensed Professional Engineer? If yes, what states are you licensed in? How many years of MEP consulting engineering experience do you have? What types of projects have you supported: commercial, healthcare, higher education, government, industrial, infrastructure, or other? Do you have experience with HVAC and plumbing design for construction documents? Are you comfortable reviewing drawings, calculations, and specifications for QA/QC? What software do you use regularly, including Revit, AutoCAD, Trace, HAP, or other calculation tools? What is your typical hourly rate and weekly availability? title: LLC Registration Website Developer
desc: LLC CoPilot is a side hustle website that helps people form LLCs in multiple U.S. states. Think a leaner, faster version of LegalZoom — customers select a state, review filing requirements, fill out a form, pay, and we handle the filing manually on the backend. The good news: the hard part is already done. - Full landing page design is complete (HTML/CSS) - Multi-step LLC filing app is built (state selector, intake form, order review flow) - All 6 state data sets are populated (VA, MD, DC, TX, FL, NY) - Complete task list and spec is ready for you Your job is purely integration and backend — wiring everything together so it works in production. --- WHAT NEEDS TO BE BUILT Phase 1 — Supabase database setup (~3–4 hrs) - Create orders, customers, states, and renewals tables - Set up Row Level Security (RLS) policies - Seed state data from existing dataset Phase 2 — Stripe integration (~4–5 hrs) - Wire Stripe Checkout for 3 plans: Basic ($149), Standard ($249), Premium ($399) - Set up recurring price for annual renewal add-on ($49/yr) - Build and handle checkout.session.completed webhook - Enable Stripe Customer Portal for renewal management Phase 3 — Backend API endpoints (~4–5 hrs) - POST /api/create-checkout — creates Stripe session from form submission - POST /api/webhook — confirms payment, saves order to Supabase, triggers emails - GET /api/orders — fetches all orders for admin view - PATCH /api/orders/:id — updates order status (pending → filed → complete) - GET /api/renewals/due — fetches upcoming renewal deadlines Phase 4 — Resend email setup (~3–4 hrs) - Customer confirmation email (order details + next steps) - Internal new order alert email (notifies us) - Status update email (when order marked as filed) - Completion email with LLC documents attached - Renewal reminder email (triggered 60 days before annual report due date) Phase 5 — Frontend wiring (~3–4 hrs) - Connect landing page CTAs to the filing app - Replace mock submit button with real Stripe checkout redirect - Add post-payment success page - Add loading states, form validation, and error handling Phase 6 — Admin dashboard (~5–6 hrs) - Password-protected /admin page - Orders table: status, state, plan, customer name, date filed - Status update controls (pending → filed → complete) - Document upload to attach filed LLC docs (triggers completion email) - Renewals view sorted by upcoming due date - Basic revenue summary (total orders, MRR, orders by state) Phase 7 — Vercel deployment + domain (~1–2 hrs) - Deploy to Vercel connected to GitHub repo - Configure all environment variables in Vercel dashboard - Connect custom domain and verify HTTPS - End-to-end test in Stripe test mode Phase 8 — QA + bug fixes (~3–4 hrs) - Full flow test: form → payment → email → admin dashboard - Mobile responsiveness check - Cross-browser check --- TECH STACK - Frontend: HTML/CSS/JS (existing) or Next.js if preferred - Database: Supabase (Postgres) - Payments: Stripe Checkout + Webhooks - Email: Resend - Hosting: Vercel - Auth (admin): Supabase Auth or simple env-variable protected route --- WHAT I'M LOOKING FOR - 2+ years of full-stack experience - Demonstrated Stripe integration in your portfolio (this is non-negotiable — please share a live example) - Comfortable with Supabase or similar Postgres/BaaS platforms - Can work independently with minimal hand-holding — the spec is detailed, you should be able to run with it - Clear English communication and regular progress updates (every 2 days minimum) - Available to start within 1 week --- HOW TO APPLY Please include the following in your proposal or I will not review it: 1. A link to a live project where you integrated Stripe payments 2. Your experience with Supabase or Postgres 3. Confirm you've read the spec — tell me which phase you'd start with and why 4. Your realistic timeline to complete all 8 phases 5. Your fixed-price bid Generic proposals will be declined. I'm looking for someone who has read this post, understands the scope, and can hit the ground running. --- NICE TO HAVE (not required) - Experience with Next.js App Router - Familiarity with U.S. LLC/legal filing services (LegalZoom, ZenBusiness, etc.) - Interest in an ongoing retainer ($100–$150/mo) for maintenance and adding new states post-launch --- This is a well-scoped project with a clear spec, existing UI, and a straightforward tech stack. If you're organized, communicate well, and know your way around Stripe and Supabase — this should be smooth. Looking forward to your proposal.
